suggestions.title: Office.jsx is a 1200-line monolith — split into sub-components
suggestions.description: Office.jsx contains Character, CharacterAvatar, ConferenceTable, WindowElement, WallClock, AwaySign, VibeShockwave all in one file. This makes it harder to maintain and causes unnecessary re-renders. Split into separate files under src/components/. CharacterAvatar alone is ~200 lines of SVG that never changes.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast shows keyboard shortcuts on mobile/touch devices
suggestions.description: The ShortcutToast component shows '⌨️ Shortcuts: 1 Office · 2 Dashboard · R Refresh · Esc Clear' on all devices including mobile where keyboard shortcuts are useless. Add a CSS media query to hide it on touch devices: @media (hover: none) and (pointer: coarse) { .shortcut-toast { display: none; } }

suggestions.title: 7 ambient particles + 8 dust mote box-shadows + coffee steam = animation overload
suggestions.description: The office floor has: 7 ambient-particle spans with infinite animations, 8 box-shadow dust motes via ::before, coffee steam with 3 animated spans, plant sway animations, star twinkle, cloud drift, and more. On lower-end devices this causes jank. Reduce ambient-particle count to 4, and use will-change: transform on animated elements. The reduced-motion media query catches some but not all of these.

suggestions.title: Activity log filter buttons lack visual feedback on mobile tap
suggestions.description: The activity filter bar buttons (All, Hires, Completions, Errors) and worker filter buttons have no :active state for mobile tap feedback. Add a quick opacity or scale transition on :active to confirm the tap registered.

suggestions.title: DESK_SCREEN_SVG is hardcoded per worker ID — won't scale
suggestions.description: The DESK_SCREEN_SVG object in Office.jsx maps individual worker IDs (carlos, maya, dave, sofia, luna) to custom SVG screen content. If a new team member is added, their desk screen is blank. Consider a generic screen SVG based on role instead of individual ID.

suggestions.title: Ghost desk '···' dust floater overlaps with nameplate on small screens
suggestions.description: The ::after pseudo-element on .desk--ghost shows '···' at top: -10px, which collides with the desk nameplate (bottom: -16px of the desk above) on mobile breakpoints where desks are closer together. Add a mobile media query to hide or reposition it.

suggestions.title: No loading state for activity log — just blank then populated
suggestions.description: While workers.json gets a LoadingSkeleton, the activity log section has no skeleton/shimmer during initial load. It goes from empty to populated with no transition. Add a simple skeleton or 'Loading activity...' text.

suggestions.title: Worker card flip (ℹ️ button) has no way to flip back on keyboard
suggestions.description: The back of the worker card has a close button, but it's not auto-focused when the card flips. Keyboard users who triggered the flip via ℹ️ button have to Tab to find the close button. Add autoFocus to the close button or trap focus within the flipped card.

suggestions.title: Footer tagline animation is jarring — pops in with no transition
suggestions.description: When the vibe changes, the footer tagline re-renders with a new key but has no CSS transition. It just pops. Add a subtle fade transition or use the same chalk-write animation from the whiteboard.

suggestions.title: Multiple Date.now() calls in render path — should use shared timestamp
suggestions.description: formatIdleDuration, formatDuration, getRelativeTime, and getTimeGroup all call Date.now() independently within the same render cycle. This can cause subtle inconsistencies (e.g., one says '59m ago' while another says '1h ago'). Create a shared 'now' via context or pass it down.

suggestions.title: Vibe sparkline in StatsBar is tiny and hard to interpret
suggestions.description: The VibeSparkline SVG is 64x14px — too small to convey meaningful trend data. The dots overlap when there are 10+ entries. Either increase the size or add a hover tooltip showing the trend summary (e.g., '3 crushing, 2 in-flow in last hour').

whats_good: Coffee corner vibe text bug appears FIXED — the CSS selectors are now using data-vibe attribute correctly
Focus indicators are working — .char:focus-visible has outline styles
The ficus plant growth mechanic tied to vibeStreak is genuinely delightful
Vibe shockwave on vibe transitions is a nice touch — feels alive
Conference table huddle feature with seated mini-avatars is creative
The reduced-motion support is comprehensive — covers most animations
summary: Score UP from 3.7 to 3.8. Previous bugs (coffee corner CSS, focus indicators) confirmed FIXED. The dashboard is genuinely fun and polished. Main concerns now are performance (animation count on low-end devices), component architecture (Office.jsx monolith), and a few accessibility gaps (shortcut toast on mobile, keyboard focus trapping on card flip). Fun factor remains a 5 — this thing has personality.
